mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held above the prior month and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, markets infer persistent policy restraint and sticky inflation; that lifts expected real rates and term premium, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL falls for two consecutive monthly prints while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower, and the 10-year yield still falls over the next 20 trading days in triggered observations, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent levels signals labor-market slack and slower wage pressure, which lowers expected growth and inflation compensation; that should pull the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-crossing episodes are followed by a higher 10-year yield in a majority of cases over the next 5 trading days, or if the average yield change is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand acceleration in RSAFS together with stronger PAYEMS indicates firmer consumption and payroll income, which raises growth expectations and expected policy normalization; the 10-year Treasury yield should rise over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in RSAFS and PAYEMS are not followed by higher 10-year yields over 60 trading days more often than not, or if the average forward yield change is zero or negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after weakness signals improving capacity utilization and reduced recession risk, which lifts real-rate expectations and term premium; the 10-year Treasury yield should rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month over month after a prior decline
failure_condition: If INDPRO rebounds but the 10-year yield falls in most triggered cases over the next 20 trading days, or if the sign of the average response is negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below their recent level signal weaker interest-sensitive demand and softer future inflation pressure; that should lower the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 5% versus its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are not followed by lower 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 40% for negative yield moves.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is high while FEDFUNDS is still below the inflation pace, real policy remains loose relative to price growth, so investors demand a higher nominal yield; the 10-year Treasury yield should rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising faster than FEDFUNDS over the last 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes are followed by flat or falling 10-year yields in a majority of cases over 20 trading days, or if the average response is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a weakening labor market, which reduces growth and inflation expectations quickly enough to move the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll losses and unemployment increases do not produce lower 10-year yields over 5 trading days, or if the forward yield change is positive in most such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 together with stronger RSAFS marks broad nominal demand acceleration, which raises expected policy restraint and term premium; the 10-year Treasury yield should rise over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-and-sales expansion episodes fail to precede higher 10-year yields over 60 trading days, or if the average forward yield response is nonpositive.
